description: Redesign the login page to be minimalist and consistent with the dashboard UI. Remove the two-column layout, decorative left panel, accessPoints grid, trustChecks list, and verbose descriptions. Replace with a single centered card using shadcn/ui components (Card, Input, Button, Label) and Tailwind CSS, matching the dark theme of the dashboard.
steps: Rewrite src/app/login/page.tsx to remove the entire inline style object (shell), the accessPoints array, the trustChecks array, and the two-column layout.
Create a minimalist single-column centered layout: full-viewport dark background (bg-background), vertically and horizontally centered content using flex/grid.

Use a Card component from @/components/ui/card containing: a small AxonRouter logo/icon header with LockKeyhole icon, the login form with Label + Input (type=password) + Button (submit), and a simple error display.
Keep the existing React logic: useState for password/error/loading, useEffect for checkAuth, handleLogin submit handler, and the loading state check. These must remain functional.

Style using Tailwind utility classes and shadcn/ui component props. Match the dashboard dark theme: bg-background for page, Card component for the form container, Input component for password field, Button component for submit. No inline style objects.

Remove all unnecessary text: no 'secure dashboard access' eyebrow, no 'Route AI requests' title, no long subtitles. Keep only: app name 'AxonRouter' as a small heading, the password input with a minimal label, the login button, and the error message area.
For the first-run scenario (hasPassword === false), show a brief note below the input like 'Default password: 12345677' in muted text rather than a long paragraph.
acceptance_criteria: Login page renders a single centered card with password input and login button
No two-column layout, no accessPoints grid, no trustChecks list
Uses shadcn/ui components: Card, Input, Button, Label from @/components/ui/
Uses Tailwind CSS classes instead of inline styles
Login functionality (password submit, error display, redirect on success) still works
findings: Node.js 22.22.3 required for build (bun fails with better-sqlite3 native module). Installed via mise. All verification passed: typecheck, lint, build (149 pages generated), and 1043 tests pass.
